abstract: Urban fires pose a critical threat to smart cities, often resulting in significant property damage and loss of life. Conventional detection methods frequently struggle with latency and accuracy, hindering effective real-time intervention. To address these challenges, this paper introduces the Smart Fire Detection System (SFDS), an advanced framework built upon an optimized YOLOv8 architecture. By leveraging deep learning, the SFDS identifies fire-specific features with high precision while minimizing false positives. The system is designed around a four-tier architecture—comprising IoT, Fog, Cloud, and Application layers—that distributes computational tasks to ensure rapid processing and near-instantaneous response times. Experimental results demonstrate that the SFDS achieves state-of-the-art performance, reaching a precision rate of 97.1% across all tested classes. Beyond fire safety, the proposed framework is scalable and adaptable for detecting other urban hazards, such as flooding or gas leaks. This makes it a versatile tool for public safety management, forest monitoring, and the development of next-generation intelligent security systems.
